assignment_description:
Advanced Folding Techniques
⍞ Materials: Folding Techniques For Designers, A4 paper (This is different from traditional, American copy paper, though it looks very similar. The measurements will be completely off if you choose to use traditional copy paper.)

Level 7 starts back at the beginning of sloyd with folding techniques. However, these folding techniques require higher-level thinking, encouraging students to envision them as ways to build structures. Paul Jackson has created videos to go along with some of the techniques, but students are encouraged to read the instructions carefully and figure these things out based on what they have learned in the previous models. While sloyd is beneficial for all students to envision geometrical forms in reality, Level 7 will be particularly interesting to the students who wish to go on to higher-level maths, engineering, design, architecture, and more.

PREP: Students will need the book and A4 paper for these models. Some models will require the paper to be cut into a square or cut in half lengthwise before beginning.

➜ READ
Look through the diagrams on p.08 and read through the Introduction p.09-015.

➜ PRACTICE
Watch the tutorial videos.
∞ Video Link: Basic Concepts
∞ Video Link: Pleating into Sixteenths
Work through the first basic concept of Linear Divisions: Sixteenths p.016-017.

If you have time remaining, work through any models you have previously completed multiple times.

assignment_description:
Advanced Folding Techniques
⍞ Materials: Folding Techniques For Designers, A4 paper

PREP: Gather and prepare supplies for this model.

➜ READ
Read about Symmetrical Repeats p.031. For Term 2, you will be working through the four basic types of two-dimensional symmetry: translation, reflection, rotation, and glide reflection. You will have two to four weeks to work through each of these types.

➜ PRACTICE
Watch video tutorials on symmetrical translations.
∞ Video Link: Symmetrical Motif 1
∞ Video Link: Symmetrical Motif 2
Work through the models on symmetrical translations p.031-034. You have two lessons to work through both of these models. If you finish with lesson time left, work through the models again to help further your understanding of these folds.

assignment_description:
Advanced Folding Techniques
⍞ Materials: Folding Techniques For Designers, A4 paper

PREP: Gather and prepare supplies for this model.

➜ READ
Read through Stretch & Skew on p.044.

➜ PRACTICE
Watch video tutorials on stretch & skew folds. Note that the video is not in English. The purpose is to see an example worked out.
∞ Video Link: Stretch & Skew
Work through the models on stretch & skew p.044-049. You have five lessons to work through all of the variation models. If you finish with lesson time left, work through the models again to help further your understanding of these folds.

➜ DISCUSS
What can you build when you put multiples of these models together?
Can you think of any buildings or structures you have seen that may be based on this fold?
